tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows hypoglycemia (low blood glucose), low HbA1c, elevated serum creatinine (suggesting impaired renal function), and significant proteinuria with ketonuria. Urine is cloudy, brown, with low specific gravity, but no evidence of infection (negative nitrite/leukocytes). Findings are suggestive of possible acute or chronic kidney dysfunction, protein-losing nephropathy, and metabolic derangement. Clinical correlation and further nephrological evaluation are recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
